title: Justicia haughtii
taxonomy: Justicia haughtii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This genus, Justicia, is part of the order Lamiales and is commonly known as the Justicia or Jacob's ladder family. The plant is a member of the Acanthaceae family, which includes around 2,000 species of flowering plants. The classification of Justicia haughtii is further divided into kingdom Plantae, and phylum Angiosperms. Further research is necessary to determine the complete classification.

intro: Justicia haughtii, also known as Wild Basil or Mexican Basil, is a perennial plant native to Mexico, belonging to the Acanthaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, boasting various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Justicia haughtii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Justicia haughtii is often prepared as a tea or infused into a decoction, or used in combination with other herbs to make tonics and syrups for the treatment of various health conditions.
